title: Benzophenone - Canada.ca
content:
- Benzophenone (for consumers)
- Benzophenone - information sheet
This substance was identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP).
- Summary of publications
- Information gathering Section 71 CMP 2023
Proposed order and RIAS
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Public comments on the proposed risk management approach
Proposed risk management approach
Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Methanone, diphenyl-
Meets one or more of the criteria
Refer to proposed risk management approach
Publication of the Proposed Code of Practice for Methanone, Diphenyl- (Benzophenone) in Paint, Stain and/or Coating Products Available to Consumers in Canada , and start of a 60-day public comment period. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I, vol 160, No. 7.
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA).
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 14 of the proposed order adding benzophenone to Schedule 1 to CEPA and start of a 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the final screening assessment. The proposed risk management approach was also published for a 60-day public comment period. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 155, No. 5 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment and the risk management scope. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 31 .
In February 2026, the Proposed Code of Practice for Methanone, Diphenyl- (Benzophenone) in Paint, Stain and/or Coating Products Available to Consumers in Canada was published for a 60-day public comment period, ending on April 15, 2026. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I, Vol. 160, No. 7 – February 14, 2026 . The objective of the code of practice is to help reduce exposure of people in Canada to benzophenone contained in certain exterior and interior paint, stain or coating products that are available to consumers to levels that are protective of human health.

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA. This notice applies to benzophenone (CAS RN 119-61-9). More information is available on the information gathering with respect to certain substances under the CMP web page.
